label: A2 — Elementary
blurb: 5 partes, 25 preguntas: imágenes, completar, opción múltiple y emparejar.

audios.title: Part 1 · Questions 1–5
audios.intro: For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C). You will hear five short conversations. Use the arrow ▶ or the numbers below to move to the next conversation.
scripts: Question one. Boy: Are you borrowing a camera from the media desk, Anna? Girl: I thought about it, but I'm taking out a book about photography instead. Boy: That's cheaper if you drop it!
Question two. Boy: Shall we study at the café this afternoon? Girl: It's too noisy there, and it's cold in the park. Let's go to the library. Boy: Good idea, it's quiet there.
Question three. Woman: Are you walking to the library? Man: Yes — it was raining this morning, but it's lovely and sunny now. Woman: Take your time, then.
Question four. Mum: You look worried, Tom. Boy: I left my umbrella at the library. I've got my keys and my phone in my bag, but the umbrella isn't there. Mum: We can get it tomorrow.
Question five. Girl: Does the library close at half past four today? Boy: No, that's on Sundays. Today it's open until seven o'clock. Girl: Great, we have lots of time.

questions.q: What is the girl borrowing from the library?
What did Tom leave at the library?
What time does the library close today?

audios.title: Part 2 · Questions 6–10
audios.intro: You will hear a librarian telling students about the school book club. For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one word or a number or a date or a time.
scripts: Listen to a librarian talking about the school book club. Librarian: Hello everyone. The book club meets every Thursday after school, in room twelve. This month we are reading a story called 'The Silver Boat'. New members pay two pounds for the whole year. Please bring a pencil and your library card to every meeting. If you have any questions, ask Mrs Porter at the desk.

questions.label: Bring a pencil and your library ____

audios.title: Part 3 · Questions 11–15
audios.intro: You will hear Lucy talking to her friend Sam about the new town library. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Lucy: Hi Sam, have you been to the new library in town? Sam: Not yet. Is it near the station? Lucy: It was going to be there, but they built it next to the park in the end. Sam: When did you go? Lucy: On Wednesday, after my piano lesson. Sam: What's it like? Lucy: The children's room is nice, but the best thing is the comics section — it's huge. Sam: Can you borrow films there? Lucy: Yes, but only for three days. Books are for two weeks. Sam: Let's go together on Saturday morning. Lucy: I help my dad in the mornings — let's go after lunch instead. Sam: Fine by me.

questions.q: When did Lucy visit the library?
What does Lucy like best about the library?
How long can you keep a film?
When will Lucy and Sam go to the library?

audios.title: Part 4 · Questions 16–20
audios.intro: You will hear a girl called Grace talking about reading. For each question, choose the correct answer (A, B or C).
scripts: Grace: My aunt gave me my first real book when I was seven, and I've loved reading ever since. I usually read in bed at night, because the house is quiet then. My friends prefer stories about sport, but I always choose adventure stories. Last month I read a book that was five hundred pages long — it took me three weeks to finish! Now I want to enter the library's reading competition. You have to read six books before the holidays, and I've already finished two.

questions.q: Who gave Grace her first book?
What kind of stories does Grace choose?
How long did the long book take her to read?
How many competition books has Grace finished?

audios.title: Part 5 · Questions 21–25
audios.intro: You will hear Nora talking to her mum about books her friends borrowed from the library. What did each person borrow? For each question, choose the correct answer.
scripts: Nora: Everyone in my class borrowed something different from the library today. Jack took a comic — he loves the pictures. Emma chose a cookbook, because she wants to make a cake for her mum's birthday. Leo borrowed a ghost story; he says it isn't scary at all. Ruby got a travel book about Italy, ready for her holiday. And Daniel took an animal story about a lost dog — he says it's the best book ever.
